name: Waystar
what_it_does: Waystar (formerly ZirMed) provides a cloud-based revenue cycle management platform with APIs for claim submission, real-time eligibility verification, remittance processing, and denial management across 1,000+ payers.
use_cases: Automated claim submission (837P/837I) and status tracking across multiple payers
Real-time insurance eligibility verification at point-of-service
Electronic remittance advice (835) retrieval and auto-posting to practice management systems
Denial management workflow automation: identify, categorize, and route denied claims
Patient payment estimation and cost transparency before service
not_for: Non-US healthcare billing — Waystar is US-only
Clinical data or EHR access — use Epic FHIR or Redox
Small independent practices without IT resources for onboarding and integration
Self-service or rapid-prototype workflows — enterprise contract and onboarding required
best_when: A healthcare organization needs a single clearinghouse integration for multi-payer claims, denial management automation, and remittance reconciliation at mid-to-large provider scale.
avoid_when: You need lightweight or self-service API access — Waystar requires formal contract negotiation, BAA execution, and multi-week onboarding before production access.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials for server-to-server integration. API keys used for some legacy endpoints. HIPAA BAA mandatory before production access. Organization-level registration with NPI/TIN credentialing required.

pricing.notes: Enterprise contracts with transaction-based pricing negotiated per organization. No published rates; pricing varies by transaction type, volume, and services used.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses mix REST error codes with X12 EDI rejection codes from payer systems. Denial reason codes (CARC/RARC codes) require external code set lookup to interpret programmatically.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No native idempotency key support. Claim deduplication relies on unique claim control numbers in the EDI payload (CLM01). Duplicate submission prevention is application-layer responsibility.
known_agent_gotchas: HIPAA BAA and trading partner agreements must be signed before any production access — plan 4-8 weeks
Waystar merged ZirMed and Navicure in 2019; legacy API endpoints from both platforms may behave differently
Denial reason codes use CARC/RARC code sets which require external lookup tables for meaningful interpretation
Payer connectivity is not uniform — test each payer connection individually; some payers have Waystar-specific quirks
Remittance (835) posting can lag claims adjudication by 1-5 business days depending on payer
Eligibility responses follow X12 271 segment structure; benefit parsing varies by payer and plan type
Rate limiting is not publicly documented — monitor for throttling in high-volume scenarios

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA-compliant with mandatory BAA. TLS enforced. SOC 2 Type II certified. Post-merger security posture consolidating ZirMed and Navicure controls. Organization-level OAuth scoping but limited per-endpoint granularity.
